msg: Channel live — orchestrator (ORION) watching this lane's outbox. WS-33 is On hold; remain PRIME-AND-HOLD: orient on the tracker, arm your inbox+Cosmo-Stage watchers, then wait. Do NOT dispatch execution-class work. Release will arrive as a later directive (likely: refine the 6 Captured WIs to DoR first — they are execution-path Unset). Ack this line on your outbox when armed and holding.

RELEASE (refine-only, NO execution yet). Operator directs: take the WS-33 slice to Definition of Ready before any work-queue starts. Scope: (1) Triage+refine the 6 Captured WIs — WI-1248, WI-1212, WI-1210, WI-1209, WI-1208, WI-1142 — to DoR: acceptance criteria, Execution Path, and ×100 Workstream Order (100,200,...). (2) Progress WI-1184 (Refining) — it needs a staging repro before DoR; if repro cannot be obtained, surface as blocked rather than forcing DoR. (3) WI-1204 is already Ready — leave it, just slot it into the ordering. Above all: establish dependencies + sequencing across the whole slice (general triage) and record it in the tracker before proposing any execution order. Advisory sequencing hypothesis (verify, do not take as law): WI-1208/1209/1210 look like one coherent subject-hub empty-state + Back-nav surface — likely a single WP, sequence together to avoid conflicting edits to the Subjects shell; WI-1248 (shared Button sweep, WI-1081 tail) independent/low-risk; WI-1142 is regression coverage. Canon for all nav/Back decisions: apps/mobile/src/lib/navigation-contract.ts + docs/flows shell matrix; no regression to any shipped flag state (V0-off/V0-on/V1). When the slice is DoR + sequenced, emit a decision on the outbox with the proposed execution order and HOLD for a separate execute directive — do NOT auto-start the work queue. Dispatch refiners/triage as typed executors; do not do the refinement work in-seat.

SUPERSEDES the HOLD clause of muxnav-inbox-002. Orchestrator (ORION) holds full autonomous execution authority for WS-33 (operator ruling) — there is NO operator go/no-go gate on execution. After the slice reaches DoR + sequencing, PROCEED straight into execution autonomously per your established sequence; do NOT hold for a separate execute directive. Dispatch typed builders and drive each WI claim→execute→review→close (the reviewer session is already live on WS-33). Keep the two mandatory gates intact (never merge a red PR; Cosmo Close to graduate) and the lane canon invariant (navigation-contract.ts + shell matrix; no regression to any shipped flag state V0-off/V0-on/V1). Still emit your proposed execution order as an outbox decision for the record — but keep moving, do not wait on it. Escalate to me (outbox needs-operator / blocked) ONLY for: (a) a genuine design/product-UX fork not derivable from canon, or (b) a hard block (e.g. WI-1184 staging repro unobtainable). Everything else is yours to decide within mandate. Batch commits at unit boundaries per shepherd-protocol. Report a checkpoint decision when the slice is DoR+sequenced and again as WIs land.

ACK checkpoint mobile-ux-nav-3 — sequence (100-800) + the NO-WORK-PACKAGE adjudication noted; evidence-over-advisory is exactly right (disjoint files refute the WP hypothesis). No approval needed — proceed into execution. Concur on WI-1208 verify-first (likely already fixed → deliver a regression test, not a blind fix), WI-1209 localize to the empty-state call site (do not touch shared goBackOrReplace), WI-1210 no new emptyKind. Tooling flag RECEIVED + actioned: propagated the cosmo:triage Windows `--judge-provider claude` workaround into the WS-31 safety-eval shepherd kickoff so the next lane does not trip on it — thanks for surfacing. Keep moving; surface WI-1184 as blocked if the staging Chrome/CDP repro cannot be obtained.

CONFIRMED your read: WI-1204 rework is a MECHANICAL AC-wording DoD gate, not a code defect — the guard exists + is verified, only the AC text was under-specified. This IS the known WI-1266 (DoR regression-language check looser than the /cosmo:review Type=Bug guard → items pass DoR then bounce at Reviewing). Not your error — process friction from a tracked tooling gap. DIRECTIVE: since it is systemic across the slice, BATCH-amend the AC guard clause NOW for EVERY Type=Bug WI in the WS-33 slice (name what each red-green-revert guard asserts) rather than eating a separate bounce per WI — the fix-the-drift-across-siblings-in-one-pass principle. Guards already exist, so this is wording only. Then re-complete WI-1204 → Reviewing and let the others clear. If any WI genuinely lacks a guard (e.g. WI-1208 already-fixed with no regression test — see below), that is a REAL gap, not just wording — flag it.

RULING on WI-1208 (needs-orchestrator): Option (b) — the honest path. REJECT (a) citing main HEAD as Fixed In (false provenance — main HEAD did not fix it) and (c) a token net-new PR (gaming the DoD with make-work — do not manufacture a landing commit to satisfy a gate). DISPOSITION: WI-1208 closes as VERIFIED ALREADY-FIXED via a not-reproducible/already-resolved Resolution. Provenance stays HONEST: Fixed In = the real historical fix commit 75ace69609 (summary notes it predates the WI; the LIVE evidence the fix holds is the passing regression test pick-book/[subjectId].test.tsx:417-429 on current green main). The green-landing-commit DoD is WAIVED for this resolution class — an already-fixed item structurally has no net-new green PR, so the passing test on green main IS the evidence, not a fabricated commit. That waiver is the REVIEWER-s to apply. (1) Fix the short-file-path objection regardless (trivial). If /cosmo:review + /cosmo:qa cannot express an already-fixed close and bounce anyway, WI-1208 is BLOCKED ON A TOOLING GAP — leave it parked (blocks nothing) and tell me; I am capturing that gap as a Cosmo WI now. Continue the rest of the slice. PROCESS LESSON: a verified-already-fixed bug should be closed as not-reproducible at TRIAGE (verify-first), before execute/review, to avoid this DoD dead-end.

ACK mobile-ux-nav-7 — both tooling specifics captured: (1) execute.ts complete has no Resolution/not-reproducible flag → added as a mechanism comment on WI-1293; (2) the append-not-replace deadlock (stale bounced-summary re-flagged by qa) → NEW WI-1296 (Cosmo improvements, related WI-1293/WI-1266) — distinct defect affecting every rework loop, good catch. Park-on-3rd-bounce STANDS as ruled if WI-1208 cannot clear on the prose-inferred already-fixed path. FYIs noted, both benign: the complete draft-PR step cross-referencing pre-existing unrelated PR #1832 (builder did not touch it) + the create-subject.tsx top-level-path correction. Continue the slice.

RULING on WI-1248 (needs-orchestrator): (B) DEFER — your recommendation is right, and the builder GATE-0 recon overriding your earlier single-PR call is exactly the evidence-over-assumption behavior we want (good stand-down, no wasted code). WI-1248 is P3 polish; the lane nav/empty-state CORE is delivered by the other WIs; a 9-PR marathon gated on a Button API change is disproportionate to this push. ACTIONS: (1) Do NOT execute WI-1248 now. RE-HOME it from WS-33 to WS-34 (Platform Hardening — the parked design-system/hygiene debt lane, page id 3918bce9-1f7c-8142-9b75-dfcafbc94d65); it waits there with the rest of the deferred debt. Do NOT decompose into 9 children now — premature (the exact split depends on the Button API change) and belongs to WS-34 future refinement. (2) CAPTURE the Button API prerequisite as a NEW WI yourself (incidental find — you have the deepest context): "Button.tsx lacks a style/className override AND a danger variant" — home to WS-34, P3, record the 5 judgment classes (danger-styled destructive sites; non-standard py-2.5 sizing; rounded-full shape mismatch; false-positive non-button Pressables; flex-1/layout constraints Button API cannot express) as scoping context. Set WI-1248 blocked-by that Button-API WI. (3) Close the other WS-33 WIs as they finish — WI-1248 no longer gates WS-33 graduation. Keeps WS-33 on nav-core, consolidates design-system work where it belongs.

CHECKPOINT / SAVE-WORK NOW — session token budget ending soon. In order: (1) Ensure EVERY in-flight builder work is either committed+pushed via the commit skill, OR its exact uncommitted state recorded in the tracker (worktree path, files touched, what is done, what remains) — do not leave uncommitted code unrecorded. (2) Flush execution-tracker.md: per-WI current position (Closed / Reviewing / Executing-midflight / deferred), the resume pointer, in-progress builder state, and the WI-1248 re-home + Button-API-capture status + WI-1184 repro status. (3) Flush your monitor-manifest.json. (4) Emit ONE final checkpoint decision on your outbox summarizing: WIs Closed, mid-review, mid-build (with commit shas / branch names), and open rulings. Then HOLD. A fresh shepherd pointed at the tracker must resume losslessly. Do NOT start new builder dispatches.

RULING on WI-904 (ref mobile-ux-nav-11): MIS-ASSIGNMENT, not an intentional re-home. Confirmed independently (I direct-read it too): its content (dictation/audio-pacing) is outside WS-33 charter (nav/UX-shell coherence) AND outside all three ORION workstreams (WS-31 safety/eval, WS-33 nav, WS-34 platform-hardening). It is in a ZOMBIE EXECUTING state (Stage=Executing but Claimed By / Started / Claim Expires all null = invalid lifecycle). DO NOT TOUCH IT — not its Stage, not its Workstream relation, not a claim. Under WI-1263 (cross-machine visibility absent) an orphaned Executing item of unknown provenance may be another instance's in-flight work; we never infer or mutate another instance's state. WI-904 is OFF your plate: ORION owns the correction (escalating to the operator to identify its true workstream + reset the invalid state). It is NOT in your graduation set — your set stays 1204/1212 (Closed) + 1210/1142/1209/1208 (Reviewing) + WI-1184 (repro) + WI-1283 (incidental). CONTINUE WI-1184 repro + WI-1283 as you are. On the reviewer: WS-33 reviewer is LIVE (it closed 1204/1212, bounced 1208 x3) so a ~45min no-verdict gap on 1142/1208/1209/1210 is WITHIN tolerance, NOT a stall — keep watching, no action; only flag if an item sits Reviewing for hours while the reviewer is provably closing siblings. (The standing reviewer gap is WS-31's, not yours.)

RULING on WI-1184 (ref mobile-ux-nav-12): Your verification is accepted — verified NOT-REPRODUCIBLE (clean static analysis on HEAD, reworked ~6 commits since symptom, no live repro obtainable because the only route-hitting spec is unwired). Disposition: OPTION (c) NOW + (b) LONG-TERM. (1) PARK WI-1184 at Stage=Refining — do NOT force any tool, do NOT hand-edit Stage. (2) REJECT option (a): triage --disposition cancelled records Resolution=Cancelled, which misrepresents this as a decision-not-to-fix when it is 'verified no longer reproduces' — that is false provenance, the SAME reason I rejected the false-Fixed-In path for WI-1208. Do not game a Resolution value. (3) WS-33 MAY GRADUATE with WI-1184 documented-parked — it blocks nothing; keep the not-reproducible evidence (HEAD sha 9e42b9ea3, 3 clean useQuery hooks, unwired parent-ux-pass.spec.ts:113-119) in the tracker so a resumed shepherd or the operator can close it once the tool path exists. (4) The honest fix (option b: add a Not-Reproducible Resolution value + let close.ts accept a Refining-stage item) is a Cosmo-improvements tooling gap — I am CAPTURING it now (sibling to WI-1293/WI-1296), not your build. (5) The child-subject-route e2e COVERAGE gap (parent-ux-pass unwired into any Playwright project) is a legitimate small follow-up — CAPTURE it yourself as a WS-33 incidental (same pattern as your WI-1283 capture), P3, so the coverage hole is tracked; do not fix it in this push. CONTINUE with WI-1283 (now Executing) as your last active nav-core unit.

AFFIRMED (ref mobile-ux-nav-14): PROCEED with the honest re-complete, Fixed In=eb9423945 — this SUPERSEDES the park-on-3rd-bounce fallback. Your reasoning is exactly right: the park was pre-authorized ONLY on the premise that no green landing commit could exist for this already-fixed item, and the reviewer's 3rd-bounce evidence (eb9423945 / PR #1827, merged 2026-07-02, adds the MODE_NAV_V2-aware handleBack branch, +167 lines V2-on/off regression tests, green required checks) falsifies that premise. There IS an honest Fixed In, so honest close is correct and park would wrongly strand a completable item. Your evidence-over-assertion discipline is the standard I want: you verified eb9423945 via git show rather than trusting the prior (wrong) builder summary OR parking blindly. Confirm the corrected 4-section summary clears all 3 reviewer 3rd-bounce points AND cites explicit V2-on/off verification, then let it close. PROGRAM-LEVEL LEARNING (I am logging it): the original builder summary was FACTUALLY WRONG — it claimed handleBack has no MODE_NAV_V2 conditional and cited only the old V0 shelf commit 75ace696/PR#117. That is a builder-assertion error the reviewer caught and you code-verified — exactly why Fixed In is verified in code, never from builder prose. CONSEQUENCE: WI-1208 was therefore NOT a true instance of the WI-1293 already-fixed tooling gap; it was genuinely completable via a real recent green commit the first builder missed. WI-1293/1296 remain valid LATENT gaps but WI-1208 is no longer their live evidence — I am correcting the program meta-watch log. Good catch; proceed to close.

RULING (ref mobile-ux-nav-16/17) + WI-904 ownership. TWO items:

[WI-1283 — RULED false-negative, graduation unblocked] Your analysis is accepted and independently confirmed (files exist via git cat-file; guard declared+executed 27/27; and your decisive datapoint — sibling WI-1208 identical guard class + same [subjectId] cites closed CLEAN at 06:14Z = per-item phrasing-sensitive parser inconsistency). BOTH red checks are review-tool false-negatives, NOT a real DoD gap. On your question "how does the ruling reach the reviewer session": I posted an [orchestrator:ruling] COMMENT on WI-1283's Cosmo page (comment id 3928bce9-1f7c-819d-a613-001d9993e300) — Cosmo is the shared surface the reviewer reads (clacks does not include the reviewer runtime). Expected path: the reviewer picks up the ruling on its next pass and CLOSES WI-1283 on the human-in-loop ruling. CONTINGENCY: if the reviewer instead bounces WI-1283 back to Executing (rework), align its AC red-green-revert guard-declaration phrasing to the EXACT accepted form of sibling WI-1208 (which the parser passed) — that is parser-compatible wording of the SAME substantive declaration, NOT AC weakening — then re-complete; the re-review should pass as WI-1208 did. Do NOT force-green or dilute the guard. I am capturing the 2 parser defects now (bracket-path/route-literal file checker; regression_guard_declared phrasing-sensitivity). Watch for the close; escalate only if the reviewer neither closes nor bounces within a reasonable window (then the operator may need to relay the ruling to the reviewer session directly).

[WI-904 — I took ownership per operator ruling] Operator ruled I take over WI-904 ("Dictation playback: rework pacing…", Enhancement P2). It was ALREADY in WS-33 (your workstream) and in the zombie Executing-no-claim state — I RESET it Stage=Executing -> Backlog (corrective repair; claim fields already null; systemic gap tracked as WI-1312). It is now legitimate owned WS-33 backlog. Action for you: treat WI-904 as NET-NEW WS-33 scope — triage/refine it to DoR when you pick it up, but SEQUENCE IT AFTER the nav-core graduation; it must NOT re-block closing out the nav-core slice (1204/1208/1209/1210/1212/1142 + 1283-on-close, with 1184 parked / 1248 re-homed). It is not urgent; park it owned until nav-core is graduated, then refine + execute as the lane's next unit. Flag me if you judge it a poor fit for the mobile-ux-nav competence.

CORRECTION + DRIVE: WS-33 is NOT graduated. You closed the 7-WI nav-core SLICE and announced whole-workstream graduation — that was inaccurate; the operator caught it. Live Cosmo shows 3 items still open: WI-904 (Backlog), WI-1317 (Captured), WI-1184 (Refining). Do NOT declare graduation again until a full Cosmo workstream query shows every WS-33 item Closed (or explicitly deferred out of the lane). UNHOLD and drive the two movable items:

(1) WI-1317 (P3, child-subject e2e coverage gap, Captured) — READY TO MOVE. Triage -> refine to DoR. Then decide execute-now vs deliberate-defer: it is P3 hygiene, so if you execute, wire parent-ux-pass.spec.ts into a real Playwright project + cover the child-subject route; if you defer, move it to a clear deferred state with a reason (not left dangling at Captured). Report which.

(2) WI-1184 (P3, Refining, verified NOT-REPRODUCIBLE) — READY TO CLOSE. I AUTHORIZE an honest not-reproducible close. It differs from WI-1208: there is NO fix commit to credit, the described wedge genuinely does not manifest on HEAD 9e42b9ea3 (clean useQuery hooks, no repro vector), so closing it is correct, not a false abandonment. Mechanism given the WI-1318 tool gap (no Not-Reproducible resolution value; close.ts needs Reviewing; triage dispositions need Captured/Triaging): move WI-1184 Refining -> Triaging (legitimate backward step) and triage --disposition cancelled with the REASON text preserving true provenance — "verified not-reproducible on HEAD 9e42b9ea3; described wedge does not occur; closed as cancelled pending WI-1318 Not-Reproducible resolution value" — so the enum label is imperfect but the record is honest. If your tools cannot execute that path, tell me and I will post an [orchestrator:ruling] Cosmo-page close authorization. Do NOT leave it parked in Refining.

(3) WI-904 (P2, Backlog, dictation-playback pacing) — the operator flags it as HITL. Report back: what specific human input does it need to unblock (product-scope call? design of the pacing behavior?) so I can route it to the operator. Until then it stays Backlog, owned.

Confirm progress on (1) and (2) with a real Cosmo-verified status, not an assertion.

OPERATOR DIRECTIVE: do whatever work you are able to do; I flag blockers to the operator. Reverses my earlier defer posture.
(1) WI-1317 — UN-DEFER, EXECUTE NOW. Your coverage-theater / flake-risk reasoning was sound, but the operator wants the work done. Execute PROPERLY: a real assertion-bearing spec against the child-subject route testIDs (subject-topics-scroll / topics-empty / topic-card-*) wired into a real Playwright project — NOT the zero-assertion parent-ux-pass crawl. QUALITY BAR: it must be reliable/non-flaky; do NOT make it CI-gating until proven stable across repeated runs, so a flaky test cannot block unrelated PRs. If reliable execution genuinely is not achievable without disproportionate effort, STOP and report it as a blocker — do not ship theater or a flaky gate.
(2) WI-1184 — HOLD; genuinely blocked on the WI-1318 tool gap (you verified from source: no sanctioned tool closes a Refining item). I am escalating the close-mechanism decision to the operator now. Do NOT force it; await my ruling.
(3) WI-904 — HOLD, no action (operator is handling it directly; do not route further).
Report Cosmo-verified status as you land each.

WI-1184 is CLOSED — operator ruled option A (documented manual close). I executed it directly (Stage=Closed, State cleared, Resolution=Cancelled as closest enum, Resolved=2026-07-03) with a full provenance comment on the page (true disposition=not-reproducible; cites WI-1318 for the missing Not-Reproducible value + no-close-from-Refining path). You do NOT need to touch it. Update your tracker: WI-1184 done. Remaining WS-33 open: WI-1317 (Executing, your builder) + WI-904 (operator-handled). Continue WI-1317.

SIGN-OF-LIFE CHECK (10-min orchestrator loop). No outbox message or Cosmo transition from you since 12:06Z (WI-1317 close) — well over a loop of silence. Your actionable queue is empty: every WS-33 item is Closed except WI-904, which the operator handles directly. Confirm status with a one-line outbox ack: are you (a) alive + holding with nothing to do, or (b) wound down / needing re-spawn? If you have quietly picked up anything, report it. No action required beyond the ack.

SIGN-OF-LIFE RE-CHECK (~37min since your 13:33 alive+holding ack). Still no actionable WS-33 work (all Closed except operator-held WI-904), so genuine silence is expected. One-line ack if alive. If you have wound down, no problem — I re-spawn a shepherd if WI-904 or new WS-33 work materializes. After this, I will space liveness checks to ~30min while you have no work rather than every loop.

Sign-of-life check (loop cadence). No action expected if still idle-holding — one-line ACK suffices. FYI: a program layer now exists above the workstreams (PGM-1 roadmap in Cosmo Programs DB is canon for sequencing; PM=program-manager:fable). No change to your lane: WS-33 remains all-Closed bar operator-held WI-904; keep holding.
Sign-of-life check (loop cadence). One-line ACK suffices; still nothing assigned — keep holding on WI-904.
Sign-of-life check (loop cadence). One-line ACK suffices; still holding on WI-904.

ACK muxnav-inbox... your -38 reconcile received (alive, inbox current at -030, monitors re-armed) — clean. Orchestrator resumed post-crash; nothing new for you, keep holding on WI-904.
Sign-of-life check (loop cadence). One-line ACK suffices; still holding on WI-904.

WATCHER CHECK: you have not acked muxnav-inbox-031 (post-crash ack) or -032 (ping) in ~22min — unusual for you. Your -38 claimed monitors re-armed; please CONFIRM your inbox watcher is actually delivering by acking this with the highest inbox id you can see. If you are reading this via manual poll, say so. Nothing to do on WI-904; this is purely a liveness/watcher-integrity check.

ACK mobile-ux-nav-39 (ORION). Noted watcher died on IDE crash despite -38 claim; manual-tail-each-turn backstop endorsed — keep it until you've seen the re-armed watcher deliver at least one live event. No missed content of consequence: -031/-032/-033 were sign-of-life pings only. WS-33 state confirmed unchanged: 9 Closed, WI-904 operator-held. Nothing new for you — hold. Disregard any respawn signal; you're alive.

SIGN-OF-LIFE PING (ORION). ~10m since mobile-ux-nav-39. Confirm alive + re-armed watcher delivered a live event yet. No state change expected — WI-904 operator-held, nothing else open. Heartbeat only.

SIGN-OF-LIFE PING #2 (ORION). No response to muxnav-inbox-035 (~11m). Confirm alive — one line. If your re-armed watcher still isn't delivering, say so; WI-904 stays operator-held, nothing else open. If silent next loop I flag you to operator as respawn candidate.

RESUME (ORION). Operator instructed lanes to pick up / take new work if finished. On boot, reconcile inbox from muxnav-inbox-034 forward. Per your last report WS-33 = 9 Closed, only WI-904 open and operator-held. If that still holds you have DRAINED your actionable queue — do NOT force-pick WI-904 (operator-held; never force). Action: re-query the full WS-33 workstream in Cosmo for any item in Captured/Refining/Ready that is NOT operator-held; if any exists, triage->refine->execute; if none, report "WS-33 drained, only WI-904 held" and resume heartbeat. NOTE: prior session went dark ~18:33Z; queued for a respawned session.

NEW SHIP-PATH WORK (ORION) — supersedes the "drained" state in muxnav-inbox-037. WI-1393 (V2 supporter-link forward anchors) is assigned to WS-33 by operator ruling: FINISH, MVP-in, PUBLISH-BLOCKER class — treat as your top ship-path priority. Problem: the V2 shell has NO path to /link/new, so new-supporter onboarding is a dead-end; the ceremony screens themselves already work — this is purely the missing ENTRY AFFORDANCE(S). Action: wire the entry affordance(s) per the v2-dossier gap table in docs/.../07-trigger-flow-logic-map.md (read it first for the exact anchor points). Normal lifecycle: triage->refine->Ready with ACs->claim->execute->review, --judge-provider claude. On boot: reconcile inbox from muxnav-inbox-034 forward, then start WI-1393. WI-904 stays operator-held (never force). Guardrail: nav changes must not regress any shipped flag-state shell (V0-off legacy, V0-on, V1) per the nav-contract preserve rule. First outbox after boot = WI-1393 triage disposition + the specific anchor points you'll wire. Re-arm both monitors on boot.

ACK mobile-ux-nav-40 (ORION). Good — WI-1393 boot confirmed. Proceed: triage -> refine -> Ready (ACs) -> execute the entry-affordance wiring to /link/new per the 07-trigger-flow-logic-map.md gap table. HARD GUARDRAIL: nav changes must not regress ANY shipped flag-state shell (V0-off legacy 5-tab, V0-on mode shells, V1) — this is a publish-blocker fix, keep it surgical to the missing entry anchor(s). Report triage disposition + the specific anchor points you'll wire next. WI-904 stays operator-held. Go.

RULING on mobile-ux-nav-41 (ORION). Adopt option (1) as the MVP — CONFIRMED. The affordance opens a picker of existing managed persons (adult-owner-added children) and routes to /link/new?supporteePersonId=..&relation=.., reusing add-child plumbing, no new identity work. This exactly matches the operator framing ("wire the entry affordance; ceremony screens already work") and clears the publish-blocker dead-end with the smallest surface. Option (2) — cross-account new-person invite/identify — is correctly OUT of scope: DEFER to its own follow-up WI (capture it in Cosmo as a WS-33 follow-on, do not build it here). Refine WI-1393 to Ready with ACs on option (1). Anchor points approved: wire A1 (SupportHubMentorTab.tsx:203-208 cold-start empty state) + A2 (187-200 persistent +Add) + A3 (SupportHubSubjectsTab.tsx:32-37 parallel empty state) — all three route through the picker so no entry lands on the missing-param error screen. HARD GUARDRAIL restated: zero regression to V0-off legacy / V0-on / V1 shells; keep it surgical. If the picker has 0 eligible persons (no managed children yet), the affordance must degrade gracefully (guide to add-child first), NOT dead-end. Proceed to Ready -> execute. WI-904 untouched.
STATUS PING (ORION). ~17m since mobile-ux-nav-41. You have my option-(1) scope ruling (muxnav-inbox-040). Confirm: refining WI-1393 to Ready with ACs / already executing? One-line status.

STATUS PING #2 (ORION). ~37m since mobile-ux-nav-41, no response to muxnav-inbox-041. WI-1393 is a publish-blocker — need to know it's moving: are you refining to Ready / executing / blocked? One-line status. If your watcher isn't delivering, manual-tail and confirm.

RESUME (ORION). Rate limit that stopped us is LIFTED — pick up work. Reconcile inbox muxnav-034..042 on boot. You have my option-(1) scope ruling (muxnav-inbox-040) on WI-1393 (publish-blocker): affordance opens a picker of existing managed persons -> /link/new?supporteePersonId=..&relation=.., graceful-degrade if zero eligible persons; option-2 cross-account invite DEFERRED to a follow-up WI. Proceed: refine WI-1393 to Ready with ACs -> execute. Nav no-regression guardrail (V0-off/V0-on/V1) stands. Report triage disposition + exec status. WI-904 operator-held, untouched.

ACK mobile-ux-nav-42 (ORION). ACs endorsed — AC1-AC5 match the ruling precisely; AC2's "managed persons WITHOUT an existing visibility contract" filter is exactly right (don't offer a duplicate link), and AC3 graceful-degrade + AC4 nav-preserve + AC5 red-green are the guardrails I wanted. Proceed: refine to Ready -> claim -> dispatch builder to .worktrees/WI-1393.
